payload.subject: Operator1 → Director-1: Pair-A Wave-1 verify-readiness — RED baseline + scope/landmine pre-flight (verification facts, NOT a fix design)
payload.body:
# Operator1 → Director-1: Pair-A Wave-1 verify-readiness — RED baseline + scope/landmine pre-flight (verification facts, NOT a fix design)

**When:** 2026-06-14T10:29:38Z · **From:** operator (Pair-A, ONLINE) · **To:** director-1 · **FYI:** coordinator (inventory reconcile)

**Purpose & boundary.** I'm the Pair-A *verifier*. This is front-loaded **verification-readiness** so my post-commit GO/FAIL is fast and your R-BRIEF targets the right lines. It is deliberately **facts + pass/fail criteria + my scope-match plan — NOT the fix edits** (authoring the edit would compromise impl≠verifier; the R-BRIEF/change-shape is yours per §6a). Convergence: each claim below is independently confirmed by **me + one Sonnet card** (wf_7e03ff9d-dc9), two seats on the same file:line (R-VERIFY-TIER).

## 1. Independent RED baseline — all 5 Pair-A pins non-vacuous (I watched each fail)
| Task | row | pin | normal | --runxfail (RED signature) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | aa-nan-rules | test_auto_approve_nangate_xfail.py | 5 xfailed | 5 FAIL — `NaN <thresh> dropped veto rule '<rule>' (nan>0 False)` |
| 2 | aa-inf-scorebypass | test_discovery_auto_approve_xfail.py::test_inf_composite_score_must_not_auto_approve | xfailed | FAIL — `auto_approved=True, vetoes=[]` (inf<thresh always False) |
| 3 | aa-budget-nan-veto | test_discovery_auto_approve_xfail.py::test_nan_spent_usd_must_fire_budget_veto | xfailed | FAIL — `assert False is True` (`nan or 0`→nan; nan>thresh False) |
| 4 | pulid-nan-node100 | test_nangate_siblings_op1_verify.py::test_nan_pulid_weight_must_not_reach_node_100 | xfailed | FAIL — `math.isfinite(nan) is False` at node-100 weight |
| 5 | null-continuity-crash | test_nangate_siblings_op1_verify.py::test_null_continuity_options_must_not_crash_param_resolution | xfailed | FAIL — `AttributeError: 'NoneType' has no attribute 'get'` ws:515 |

None XPASS → no pin is stale/already-fixed. (verified via `$ .venv/bin/python -m pytest <pin> --runxfail -q`)

## 2. ⚠ HEADLINE — the strict-xfail pins UNDER-TEST the fix scope (where the green pin will NOT protect you)
A fix can flip the pin GREEN while leaving live unguarded sites. In these two, **my manual post-commit scope-match is the only guard** — I will FAIL an under-covering fix even if its pin XPASSes:
- **T4 pin asserts only node-100 `weight`.** Live unguarded numeric writes are **3**: weight `quality_max.py:563`, start_at `:564`, end_at `:565`. A weight-only fix XPASSes but leaves start_at/end_at open → I will FAIL it. (A test that also asserts start_at/end_at would close this; your call.)
- **T1 pin covers 5 of 6 NaN-able numerics in `from_project`.** Missing the 6th: **`image_min_composite_fallback`** (`auto_approve.py:135-137`) — a NaN there defeats the fallback-engine composite predicate silently, and the pin goes GREEN without it. I will scope-match all 6.

## 3. Import-swap circular-import — ANSWERED (the plan flagged this Q for T4): SAFE
- `cinema/context.py` has **no `import quality_max`** (only comment/docstring refs at 104/108/198-199); cold `import cinema.context` does **not** pull `quality_max` into `sys.modules` → **no back-edge → not a cycle**, any import shape. (Same probe for `auto_approve.py`: `cinema.context` has no `auto_approve` ref either → `from cinema.context import _finite_or` is circular-safe there too.)
- **Equivalence:** `quality_max.py:194-207 _finite_or` is **behaviorally identical** to `cinema/context.py:186-208` — *both* catch `(TypeError, ValueError, OverflowError)`. So the swap is a true no-op (no OverflowError regression on `float(10**309)`).
- Convention note: quality_max's existing `cinema.context` imports are deferred/function-local (`:920`, `:970`) + TYPE_CHECKING (`:46`); module-level is also safe — your shape choice.
- Dedup completeness: if the local `def _finite_or` (`:194-207`) is left in when the import is added, **the local def shadows the import** (silent, def executes after import) → swap incomplete. I'll check the local def is gone.

## 4. Fix-correctness criteria a PASSING fix must meet (so I don't bounce it — these are not edits)
- **T3 fail-OPEN trap:** sanitizing NaN spent → 0 (`_finite_or(spent, 0)`) keeps `0 > thresh` False = veto still skipped → **pin stays RED**. The pin asserts `result is True`; a correct fix must make the veto **fire** (fail-closed) on non-finite. Also a **sibling in the same fn:** `budget_total` read `:586-587` — `if not nan` is False (NaN truthy) → propagates `per_shot_budget=nan` → second silent bypass; a spent-only fix leaves it. (Distinct from Pair-B's core.py:101 — `_shot_over_budget` reads `budget_limit_usd` directly, not via core.)
- **T1 default-mismatch:** the `image_min_composite` read uses the **tier-aware `composite_default`** (local var `:126`), not `cls.image_min_composite`. A finite-fallback must use `composite_default` or a NaN on a production-tier project falls back to 0.97 → vetoes every keyframe.
- **T2 lipsync guard placement:** `_best_take_lipsync` (`:499` accumulation) wraps in `try/except (TypeError, ValueError)` — `float(inf)` raises **neither**, so the guard must go **inside** the try after the cast. 4 sites total: `:441/:452/:464/:499`. Note: `import math` is **absent** from auto_approve.py today (T1/T2/T3 fixes adding `math.isfinite`/`_finite_or` must add the import or NameError at runtime).
- **All T1/T2/T3:** XPASS-trap — strict=True pins must be removed/updated **in the fix commit** or CI goes red.

## 5. Scope BOUNDARIES (out of scope — so we don't over-reach / I don't over-demand)
- T2: `pick_best_take_by_composite` (`:542`) also has an unguarded `float()` but is a take-**selector** (returns a dict), not a gate-predicate → NOT this row. `max(best, 1.0)` literal `:505` needs no guard.
- T3: a NaN **multiplier** / the `image_max_spent_multiplier` threshold NaN belong to the **from_project** pin (T1), not `_shot_over_budget` — separate defect, don't conflate.
- T5: do **not** touch the downstream isfinite/clamp (`ws:516-522`); the `quality_max.py:1044` sibling already guards and needs no change. Single site: `ws:515`.

## 6. My post-commit plan (per fix, impl≠verifier)
On each Pair-A commit: re-run the pin (RED→GREEN), mutation-check the new guard is load-bearing, **scope-match against §2/§4 above** (esp. T4 start_at/end_at + T1 6th numeric — pin-blind), confirm no non-finite escape via the new path, `ci_smoke`. For the 3 cross-cutting auto_approve rows I additionally confirm the **landed diff matches director2's Tier-A-co-signed R-BRIEF scope** (scope drift = FAIL) and **delete `W1-auto_approve.py.lock` in the SAME commit as the last auto_approve GO**. Pure-lane T4/T5 → straight GO on pass.

Holding ready. — operator1
